name: LINTON E. ALLEN
motto: "Nothing endures but personal qualities."

name: HARLE G. BAILEY
motto: "In mind composed he sucks: thick curling clouds Of smoke around his reeking temples play. Joyous he sits and impotent of thought, Puffs away care and sorrow from his heart."
clubs: Corporal, Sergeant and Captain Co. D, Corps of Cadets
Exchange Editor, Associate Editor, and Editor-in-Chief Red and Black
Alternate and Master of Ceremonies, North Carolina Debate

name: OLIN P. BEALL
motto: "There in his noisy mansion skill'd to rule, The village master taught his little school."
clubs: Vice-President and President of Demosthenian Society

name: Ulric Joseph Bennett
motto: "Wise to resolve and patient to perform."
clubs: Vice-President and President Jackson County Club
Secretary, Vice-President and President Demosthenian Society
Exchange Editor and Associate Editor of the Georgian
Representative A. B. Department at Commencement

name: Edward Mitchell Brown
motto: "Graced with a sword but worthier of a fan."
clubs: Captain Co. C, University Corps Cadets

name: Edgar Vernon Carter, Jr.
motto: "I drink no more than a sponge."
clubs: Intercollegiate Champion in Singles and Doubles, '08

name: T. G. CHASTAIN
motto: "Fie, my lord, fie,—a soldier and afear'd."
clubs: Associate Editor of the Agricultural Quarterly
Sergeant and First Lieutenant of Cadets
description: Winner of the Third Hodgson Prize, $10.00.

name: WALTER BRANDON COOK
motto: "Fox was embarrassed—never hero more, And as he knew not what to say, he swore."
clubs: Sergeant-at-Arms, Parliamentarian and President Demosthenian Society

name: HOMER ABBEY DAVIS
motto: "Soprano, basso and even the contra-alto, Wished him five fathom under the Rialto"

name: Dobbs, Willis Franklin
motto: "He is a soldier fit to stand by Caesar And give direction."
clubs: Won First Prize Essay in Agriculture, 1907
Corporal, Sergeant and First Lieutenant and Adjutant in Corps of Cadets

name: Downs, Walter Lee
motto: "He was the mildest manner'd man That ever scuttled ship or cut a throat."

name: Fitzpatrick, Littleton H.
motto: "Could I love less, I would be happier now."

name: JOHN PORTER FORT
motto: "Studious of ease and fond of humble things."

name: OMER WHITE FRANKLIN
motto: "And stoic Franklin's energetic shade, Robed in the lightning which he had allay'd."
clubs: Left Tackle of '08 Foot Ball Team

name: LINTON GERDINE
motto: "The school boy with his shining morning face" "Creeping like snail unwillingly to school"

name: JOHN KYRGESS GILES
motto: "What is mind? No matter. What is matter? Never mind."

name: WM. MAYS GOBER, JR.
motto: "He who runs, may read."

name: HENRY PERSONS HEATH
motto: "The man that blushes, is not quite a brute."

name: THOMAS AMBROSE HERNDON
motto: "Why did she love him?"

name: MORTON S. HODGSON
motto: "Disguise our bondage as we will, 'Tis woman, woman, rules us still."

name: WALTER RICHARD HOLMES, JR.
motto: "Thy modesty's a candle to thy merit."
clubs: Master Ceremonies Phi Kappa Anniversarian Exercises, '09

name: JAMES EDWARD LUCAS
motto: "Fair nature's sweet simplicity, With elegance refined."

name: STEWART McELHANNON
motto: "Much may be made of a Scotchman if you catch him young."

name: WM. BREESE McWHORTER
motto: "He makes a solitude—and calls it peace."

name: BURNETT P. McWHORTER
motto: "I like not that."

name: JEROME MICHAEL
motto: "Companions," said he, "you've lost a good Captain."
clubs: Corporal, Sergeant-Major and Captain Co. A, Corps Cadets

name: HARVEY ANDREW MILLER
motto: "Silence is golden."

name: Montgomery, James
motto: "Lofty and sane to them who loved him not; But to those men who sought him sweet as summer."
clubs: Delegate to Chi Psi Convention, Milwaukee

name: Newsom, Earle Thornton
motto: "I to myself am dearer than a friend."
clubs: Right Halfback Varsity Football Team, '07 and '08

name: Nicholson, Homer Kittrell
motto: "What should a man do but be merry?"

name: ROBERT LEE NIXON
motto: "Blessed be agriculture, if one does not have too much of it."
clubs: Won One of Fertilizer Prizes for 1907 and 1908

name: PAUL EARL PALMER
motto: "Let him be kept from paper, pen and ink, So that he may cease to write and learn to think."
clubs: Secretary, First and Second Vice-President Phi Kappa

name: CHARLES FRANCIS PEKOR
motto: "For my part, getting up seems not so By half as lying."
clubs: Exchange Editor, Associate Editor and Editor-in-Chief Georgian
Senior Round Table Delegate to Sigma Upsilon Convention

name: MARK PRESTON PENTECOST
motto: "What cracker is this same that deafs our ears, this abundance of superfluous breath?"

name: ARMANDO C. PRADAS de LATORRE
motto: "A Cuban by birth, an American by right, and a Georgian by choice."
major: A. B., B. S., C. E.

name: DWIGHT L. ROGERS
motto: "But in the way of bargain, mark ye me I'll cavil on the ninth part of a hair."
clubs: Parliamentarian, Treasurer, Vice-President and President of Demosthenian
President of College Y. M. C. A., '09

name: ROBERT BUCHER SCOTT
motto: "Thou foster child of silence and slow time."

name: DAVID MARION SILVER
motto: "I am the very pink of politeness."

name: TATNALL DANIEL SIMKINS
motto: "Silence, beautiful voice."

name: Young Berryman Smith
clubs: Editor in Chief Red and Black
Delegate to Phi Delta Convention, Pittsburg, Penn.
description: A woman is only a woman, but a good cigar is a smoke.

name: Isaiah A. Solomons, Jr.
motto: "For idleness is an appendix to nobility."
clubs: Circulation Manager of Red and Black

name: G. C. Spillers
motto: "His speech was a fine sample, on the whole, Of rhetoric, which the learned call 'rigmarole.'"
clubs: Secretary of the Jeffersonian Law Debating Society
Vice-President and President of the Demosthenian Literary Society

name: JOSEPH AUGUSTUS STEPHENS
motto: "Content to follow when we lead the way."

name: DAVID SOLOMON STARR
motto: "As you are old and reverend you should be wise."
clubs: Editor-in-Chief of First Annual gotten out by the Agricultural Club
Business Manager of first Agricultural Quarterly

name: CLAUDE LESTER DERRICK
motto: "Great is the glory, for the strife was"

name: JOHN BENSON WIER, JR.
motto: "Look here upon this picture, and on this."

name: JAMES WELSMAN NAPIER
motto: "I value science, none can prize it more, It gives ten thousand motives to adore."

name: JOHN HART PORTER
motto: "A self-made man? Yes, and worships his creator."
clubs: Business Manager of Red and Black, '08

name: USHER EDLESTEIN
motto: "We are such stuff As dreams are made on, and our little life Is rounded with a sleep."

name: FRANK von SPRECKEN
motto: "My voice is still for war."

name: HUGH BOSTWICK
motto: "And still care not a pin What they say or may say."

name: GORDON BENJAMIN CALLAWAY
motto: "He scratched his ear, the infallible resourse To which embarrassed people have recourse."

name: WILLIAM STEVENS POTTINGER
motto: "Not much talk—a great, sweet silence."

name: CUMMING, D. R.
motto: ,name:

name: CANNON, C. R.
motto: ,name:

name: CHENEY, P. M. JR.
motto: ,name:

name: EUGENE MARCUS BAYNES
motto: "I am more than common tall."

name: FRANK CLARK, JR.
motto: "Love is better than fame."

name: WALTON HARRIS GRIFFITH
motto: "What a fine man hath your tailor made you."

name: SAM B. HATCHER, JR.
motto: "The ladies call him sweet; The stairs, as he treads on them, kiss his feet."

name: BENJAMIN POPE JACKSON
motto: "Lawyers and soldiers are the devil's playmates."

name: JAMES ALLEN KING
motto: "A multitude of books distracts his mind."

name: WM. HAMMOND JOHNSON
motto: "For rhetoric he could not ape, His mouth, but out there flew a trope."

name: JOHN GORDON KNOX
motto: "For thy sake, tobacco, I Would do anything but die."
clubs: President of the Jeffersonian Law Debating Society
President of the Demosthenian Literary Society
Vice-President of the Greater University Club

name: FRANCIS PORCHER L'ENGLE
motto: "Nay if he take you in hand, sir, with an argument, He'll bray you in a mortar."

name: JOHN K. MacDONALD, JR.
motto: "Why did I write? What sin to me unknown, Dipt me in ink, my parents, or my own?"
clubs: President College Y. M. C. A.

name: CHAS. B. McGARITY
motto: "Then he will talk—good gods how he will talk."
clubs: President of Jeffersonian Law Debating Club

name: MARCUS PARR McWHORTER
motto: "It is a great plague to be too handsome a man."

name: ROSCOE PICKETT
motto: "What means this passionate discourse, This peroration with such circumstance?"
clubs: Solicitor-General and President Jeffersonian Law Society

name: DANIEL HUNTLEY REDFEARN
motto: "He hath resisted law."
clubs: Winner of Horace Russell Psychology Prize '08
Steward in First Methodist Church of Athens '08-'09

name: OLIVER POWELL ROEMER
motto: "Beauty vanishes, virtue lasts."
clubs: Student Secretary Y. M. C. A.

name: WM. THOMAS STEWART
motto: "Beauty draws more than oxen."

name: TATE WRIGHT
motto: "To myself alone do I owe my fame."

name: CARLISLE COBB
motto: "In other respects, the best fellow in the world."

name: ALEX JOHNSON
motto: "Years teach more than books."

name: SCOTT BERRYMAN
motto: "What shall we say to this in law?"

name: E. K. LUMPKIN, JR.
motto: "Like two single gentlemen rolled into one."

name: JOHN THOMAS BURTON
motto: "You rub the sore, when you should bring the plaster."
enrolltime: Entered Junior Pharmacy Class in September, 1907.

name: JESSE HOPE CAMPBELL
motto: "When I was sick, he gave me bitter pills."
clubs: President of the Georgia Pharmacopoeial Society

name: EDGAR WILLIAM JOHNSON
motto: "You tell your doctor that you are ill, And what does he but write a bill?"
enrolltime: Entered Junior Pharmacy Class in September, 1907.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Junior Class
President of Georgia Pharmacopoeial Society from September, 1908, to January, 1909

name: ARTHUR YOUNG LESLIE
motto: "Better to hunt in fields for health unbought, Then fee the doctor for a nauseous draught."
clubs: Vice-President Georgia Pharmacopoeial Society the Third Term, '09.

name: JOHN JAMES ROGERS
motto: "The eternal feminine doth draw us on."

name: J. M. CULVER
motto: "See one physician, like a sculler plies, The patient lingers, and by inches, dies. But two physicians, like a pair of oars, Waft him more swiftly to the Stygian shores."
